publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Local scope; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Local scope: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? If two variables share the same spelling anywhere in a program, they must refer to one global value.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Resolve each use from its lexical context and active frame, and make cross-scope state explicit rather than assuming identical spelling implies identity.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Resolve each use from its lexical context and active frame, and make cross-scope state explicit rather than assuming identical spelling implies identity.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Local scope instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Resolve each use from its lexical context and active frame, and make cross-scope state explicit rather than assuming identical spelling implies identity.
If two variables share the same spelling anywhere in a program, they must refer to one global value.

answer.explanation: Resolve each use from its lexical context and active frame, and make cross-scope state explicit rather than assuming identical spelling implies identity.
